SEARCH
Package com.nib.supernaut.ctg

Interface ISupernautGateway

All Superinterfaces:
GatewayReturnCodes
All Known Implementing Classes:
RestSupernautGateway, TcpSupernautGateway
public interface ISupernautGateway extends GatewayReturnCodes
ISupernautGateway serves as an interface for communication with a Supernaut Transaction Gateway. It provides methods for initiating interactions with the gateway, managing protocol configurations, and maintaining the connection state.

This interface extends the GatewayReturnCodes interface, which defines error codes for operations that interact with the gateway.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from interface com.nib.supernaut.ctg.GatewayReturnCodes

    astrGateway_Rc, ERROR_BASE, ERROR_CONNECTION_FAILED, ERROR_EXIT_RETRY_INVALID, ERROR_GATEWAY_BACK_LEVEL, ERROR_GATEWAY_CLOSED, ERROR_GATEWAY_EXCEPTION, ERROR_INVALID_REQUEST_TYPE, ERROR_NOT_AUTHORIZED, ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED, ERROR_NULL_DATA, ERROR_REPLY_MISMATCH, ERROR_SECURITY_VIOLATION, ERROR_UNKNOWN_REQUEST_TYPE, ERROR_WORK_WAS_REFUSED, ERROR_XA_SUPPORT_NOT_ENABLED, strINVALID_GATEWAY_RC

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Closes the connection to the Supernaut Transaction Gateway or any associated resources.

    Retrieves the currently configured SSL protocol properties used for communication with the Supernaut Transaction Gateway.

  • Method Details

    • flow

      int flow(ECIRequest eciRequest) throws IOException
      Processes a transaction with the Supernaut Transaction Gateway using the details provided in the ECIRequest object.
      Parameters:
      eciRequest - The request object of type ECIRequest containing user credentials, program details, transaction identifiers, and any additional data required for executing the transaction with the gateway.
      Returns:
      An integer representing the status or return code from the gateway, which may indicate success, failure, or specific error codes as defined in the GatewayReturnCodes and ECIReturnCodes interfaces.
      Throws:
      IOException - If an I/O error occurs during communication with the gateway.

    • setProtocolProperties

      void setProtocolProperties(Properties sslProps)
      Sets the SSL protocol properties for communication with the Supernaut Transaction Gateway. These properties are typically used to configure secure communication, such as trust stores, keystores, and SSL/TLS versions.
      Parameters:
      sslProps - A Properties object containing key-value pairs of SSL configuration properties.

    • getProtocolProperties

      Properties getProtocolProperties()
      Retrieves the currently configured SSL protocol properties used for communication with the Supernaut Transaction Gateway.
      Returns:
      A Properties object containing the SSL configuration properties. Returns an empty Properties object if no properties have been set.

    • setURL

      void setURL(String url)
      Sets the URL endpoint for the Supernaut Transaction Gateway. This URL specifies the network address and port where the gateway can be reached.
      Parameters:
      url - A String representing the URL of the Supernaut Transaction Gateway.

    • getURL

      String getURL()
      Retrieves the URL endpoint currently configured for the Supernaut Transaction Gateway.
      Returns:
      A String representing the URL of the Supernaut Transaction Gateway. Returns null or an empty string if no URL has been set.

    • isOpen

      boolean isOpen()
      Checks whether the connection to the Supernaut Transaction Gateway is currently open.
      Returns:
      true if the connection is open; false otherwise.

    • close

      void close() throws IOException
      Closes the connection to the Supernaut Transaction Gateway or any associated resources.

      If the connection is already closed, invoking this method has no effect. This method should be called to release any underlying resources or connections to avoid potential resource leaks.

      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while attempting to close the connection.
